summary.what: Fix --batch entry anchor: prefer first-parent-line commit over commits[0]
summary.why: Root cause of Laura's v0.22.0 friction — batch mode grouped a Work-item trailer's local + cross-branch commits, then naively picked commits[0] which sometimes landed on a side-branch SHA. The resulting entry was structurally valid but its anchor pointed to a side branch, which broke linear-anchor assumptions downstream
summary.how: New helper pickBatchAnchor in cmd/timbers/log_batch_anchor.go iterates the group's commits and returns the first one on HEAD's first-parent line; falls back to commits[0] when no commit qualifies (pure cross-agent debt case, where the off-first-parent-line diagnostic from v0.22.1 surfaces the situation). HEAD resolved best-effort via git.HEAD() — fails gracefully back to legacy behavior on git error rather than failing the batch run

notes: Two independent reviewers converged: first reviewer caught my original Phase 1 plan as a regression for solo-dev-feature-branch flows; second reviewer dug into Laura's transcript and identified the --batch line as the actual culprit. The fix is much smaller than the algorithm change I'd planned and addresses Laura's case directly. Diagnostic surface from v0.22.1 still ships value — it surfaces the residual cross-agent debt case where pickBatchAnchor falls back to commits[0].
